NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE FROM A TORNADO WITH TOP WINDS ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH. THREE BARNS ON ONE FARM WERE DAMAGED AND A TELEPHONE POLE AS WELL AS SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

BETWEEN 11 AND 11:30 AM JUNE 1ST, STRONG WINDS WENT THROUGH MASON COUNTY DOWNING LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND UPROOTING SEVERAL TREES. THE MOST CONCENTRATED DAMAGE WAS REPORTED NEAR STEARNS PARK BEACH IN LUDINGTON AND IN HAMLIN TOWNSHIP EASTWARD ALONG HIGHWAY 10 WITHIN A 3-4 MILE NORTH TO SOUTH SWATH. A 48 KNOT -- 55 MPH -- WIND GUST WAS MEASURED AT 11:20 AM AT BIG SABLE POINT AS THE THUNDERSTORMS MOVED THROUGH. ADDITIONALLY, A 1.5 FOOT WATER RISE WAS OBSERVED AT THE LUDINGTON NOS GAUGE 1.5 MILES INLAND AS THE THUNDERSTORMS CAME THROUGH, WITH MINOR FLOODING REPORTED ALONG FERRY STREET IN DOWNTOWN LUDINGTON.
